# A voter goes down. The leader engages the witness to maintain commit quorum,
# then later releases it and transitions back to voter-based commits.
#
# Two voters, one witness. Quorum = 2.

setup
v1: [a1]
v2: [a1]
w
----
v1: [a1] term=a
v2: [a1] term=a

# v1 wins election. Witness vote is dropped to keep setup minimal.
campaign v1 drop=(w)
----
v1 campaigns at term b
v1: yes (self)
v2: yes
w:  (dropped)
outcome: won

# v2 goes down (partitioned from here on).
down v2
----
ok

# v1 proposes an entry.
# Matches: [v1=3, v2=0, w=0]. Quorum match = 0, not committed.
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b

append v1
----
v1 appends through b3
v2: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b leader
v2: [a1] term=b

# v1 engages the witness. Now it counts toward quorum.
# Matches: [v1=3, v2=0, w=3]. Quorum match = 3, committed!
engage v1
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, -) acked=(b3, engaged)
v1: [a1 b2 b3] term=b committed=b3 leader

# v2 prevotes (v1 partitioned away). The witness rejects: v2's last log entry
# a1 is from term a, which is older than the term b leader that engaged the
# witness. Without proof that a post-engagement leader was elected, granting a
# vote could let v2 form a quorum that misses entries the witness helped commit.
down v1
----
ok

prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
w:  no
outcome: lost

up v1
----
ok

# Propose and replicate another entry while the witness is still engaged. This
# one commits immediately (witness match advances with the leader's log).
propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b committed=b4 leader
v2: [a1] term=b

append v1
----
v1 appends through b4
v2: (dropped)
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4] term=b committed=b4 leader
v2: [a1] term=b

# v1 releases the witness, returning to voter-based commits. Note that the
# witness reflects the updated high water mark, i.e. it knows the highest
# position it has helped commit.
release v1
----
ok
w:  vote=(b, -) acked=(b4, released)

# v2 prevotes again after the release. The witness is no longer engaged, but
# its acked high-water mark b4 is still ahead of v2's last log entry a1. The
# witness won't vote for a candidate whose log doesn't cover what it has acked.
down v1
----
ok

prevote v2
----
v2 prevotes at term c
v2: yes (self)
v1: (dropped)
w:  no
outcome: lost

up v1
----
ok

# v2 comes back online. v1 proposes and replicates to v2, committing via the
# traditional v1+v2 quorum — no witness engagement needed.
up v2
----
ok

propose v1
----
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4 b5] term=b committed=b4 leader
v2: [a1] term=b

append v1
----
v1 appends through b5
v2: ok
v1: [a1 b2 b3 b4 b5] term=b committed=b5 leader
v2: [a1 b2 b3 b4 b5] term=b
